name: autopsy
title: autopsy
description:
graphical interface to SleuthKit
The Autopsy Forensic Browser is a graphical interface to the command line
digital forensic analysis tools in The Sleuth Kit. Together, The Sleuth Kit
and Autopsy provide many of the same features as commercial digital forensics
tools for the analysis of Windows and UNIX file systems (NTFS, FAT, FFS,
EXT2FS, and EXT3FS).

arguments.description: Force the program to not use cookies even for remote hosts.
arguments.raw: -C  Force the program to not use cookies even for remote hosts.

arguments.description: Force the program to use cookies even for localhost.
arguments.raw: -c  Force the program to use cookies even for localhost.

command_examples: autopsy [-c] [-C] [-d evid_locker ] [-i device filesystem mnt ] [-p port ] [addr]
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use autopsy because graphical interface to SleuthKit.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use autopsy as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `autopsy [-c] [-C] [-d evid_locker ] [-i device filesystem mnt ] [-p port ] [addr]` as documented upstream for autopsy.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair autopsy with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `autopsy --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: graphical interface to SleuthKit.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: graphical interface to SleuthKit.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install autopsy
Install with: sudo apt install autopsy
